data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Adobe's infrastructure likely utilizes a microservices architecture to handle various services and applications efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Subdivided applications into individual services with dedicated APIs, enabling independent deployment and scalability.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Adobe leverages cloud services for scalability and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Utilization of AWS or Azure for hosting and scaling, ensuring global accessibility.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Use of CDN to accelerate content delivery and improve user experience globally.
data.technical_spec: Services like Akamai or Cloudflare, offering load balancing and quick asset delivery.

data.title: Data-Driven Architecture
data.description: Architecture designed to handle large sets of user data and analytics.
data.technical_spec: Implementation of big data frameworks like Hadoop or Spark for data processing.

data.title: Containerization
data.description: Adobe uses containerization for application deployment and management.
data.technical_spec: Docker or Kubernetes for container management, providing consistency across environments.
